DEMURRER TO PROBATE CODE SEC. 850 PETITION, CALENDAR OF OCT-04-2017 THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST THREE CAUSES OF ACTION RELATING TO THE OWNERSHIP OF PROPERTY (850, QUIET TITLE, DECLARATORY RELIEF). PETITIONERS HAVE STANDING FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF BRINGING THE 850 PETITION PURSUANT TO PROBATE CODE SECTION 850(A)(2)(C) AS PARTIES CLAIMING AN INTEREST IN PROPERTY TO WHICH THE DECEDENT HELD TITLE AT THE TIME OF HIS DEATH. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE PETITIONERS ARE NOT OTHERWISE INTERESTED PARTIES TO THIS PROBATE PROCEEDING AND HAVE NO BASIS TO SUCCEED TO ANY PROPERTY THROUGH PROBATE. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR FULL TEXT.)

calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for Alyson Tufts (Petitioner).
Eric Francisconi appeared by telephone for Debra Duncan and Nicole Biddinger.
Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Beneficiary/Respondent).

The Court stated that it is pleased to see that the parties have reached settlement. Ms. Lofdahl stated that there are no objections and that consents from all of the parties have been filed. Mr. Francisconi stated that he has no objections to the form of the settlement and how distribution is being paid. The Court granted the Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. and orders distribution by way of the agreement of the parties.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For: 1) Probate Code Sections 850-859 2) Declaratory Relief 3) Quiet Title 4) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty 5) Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Fiduciary Duty 6) Elder Abuse 7) Elder Abuse 8) Probate Code Section 259 9) Constructive Trust 10) Resulting Trust 11) Constructive Fraud 12) Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Continued To Oct-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299977 The D J H Dated July 24, 1991 Trust and PES-17-300524 The Estate of James T. Pongracz.
Appearances: Eric Francesconi and Richard Bryan for Debra Duncan and Nicole Biddinger (Petitioners).
Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Beneficiary).
John Boessenecker appeared by telephone for Ramon Apodaca (Respondent).
Nancy Lofdahl also present.

The Court noted that it has now ruled on the summary judgment motion. Mr. Francesconi stated that they have met and conferred, that everything is still where it was and that they are ready for a trial date. Mr. Land requested a settlement conference. Mr. Francesconi stated that he is amenable to a settlement conference. A settlement conference is scheduled before Commissioner Pang Ly on September 11, 2018 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 603. The parties are to immediately e-mail Ms. Ly. The Petition for 1) Declaratory Relief 2) Quiet Title 3) Probate Code Section 850 4) Constructive Trust 5) Resulting Trust 6) Accounting and the Petition for 1) Probate Code Sections 850-859 2) Declaratory Relief 3) Quiet Title 4) Breach of Fiduciary Duty 5) Aiding and Abetting Breach of Fiduciary Duty 6) Elder Abuse (Civil Code 3345) 7) Elder Abuse (Welfare and Institutions Code 15610.30) 8) Probate Code Section 259
9) Constructive Trust 10) Resulting Trust 11) Constructive Fraud 12) Accounting are set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on , October 15, 2018 at 9:30 a.m., (orders for the same signed in open court/a copy of both orders and notice of trial was provided to Counsel).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For 1 Probate Code Sections 850-859 2 Declaratory Relief 3 Quiet Title 4 Breach Of Fiduciary Duty 5 Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Fiduciary Duty 6 Elder Abuse 7 Elder Abuse 8 Probate Code Section 259 9 Constructive Trust 10 Resulting Trust 11 Constructive Fraud 12 Accounting
Off Calendar Apr-17-2018 Continued To Aug-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299977 In the matter of the D J H dated July 24, 1990 Trust
PES-17-300524 The Estate of James T. Pongracz

Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for Alyson Tufts (Trustee/Executor)
John Boessenecker for and with Ramon Apodaca (Respondent)
Eric Francisconi and Richard Bryan for Nicole Biddinger and Debra Duncan (Petitioners); Mr. and Mrs. Duncan present in Court
Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Beneficiary)

The Court noted that it would be necessary to send this matter to Dept 206 for trial. Mr. Bossenecker stated that of the remaining claims, most have no witnesses to the events at issue. The Court responded that it could not rule on Motions in Limine and accordingly many witnesses might be required for the trial. It did not appear to him therefore that the trial will be able to he heard in one trial day, and the matter needed to be sent to Dept 206. The Court and Counsel discussed trial scheduling, with all Counsel being available in mid-August. The matter is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on August 13, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. Order for the same signed in open court, and a copy of the order was provided to Counsel.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Craig Blackstone. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299977 The DJH Trust and PES-17-300524 Estate of James Pongracz.

Appearances: Michael Fitzgerald appeared by telephone for Debra Duncan and Nicole Biddinger (Petitioners).
John Boessenecker for Ramon Apodaca (Respondent/Beneficiary).
Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Beneficiary).
Nancy Lofdahl for Alyson Tufts (Trustee/Executor).

Mr. Fitzgerald stated that they have not talked as to how to proceed, that he anticipates a response to the order and the 850 petitions, and that he thinks there are triable issues of fact. Mr. Fitzgerald also stated that he has taken some mini depositions of Ray and Alyson, that he has two to three hours left for each of them, that he may be locating witnesses, and that he anticipates completing discovery by the end of April and some law & motion activity. Mr. Land and Mr. Boessenecker request a trial date on the issue of whether the property is properly in the Trust or not. The Petition for Declaratory Relief, etc. and the 850 Petition are continued to April 3, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a final status hearing. The discovery cut off date is March 23, 2018. The Court expects status statements by March 27, 2018 indicating what the issues are for trial, the anticipated length of the trial, who the witnesses will be, the number of exhibits and what facts they can stipulate to. The parties are to meet and confer prior to the hearing date. The Court will then anticipate setting a trial date either here or in the trial department.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Demurrer To Probate Code Sec. 850 Petition
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called with case no. PTR-16-299977, The Matter of D J H (Dated July 24, 1990) Trust.

Appearances: George Vausher and Michael Fitzgerald for Debra Duncan and Nicole Biddinger (Petitioners). Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Respondent/Beneficiary). John Boessnecker for Ramon Apodaca III (Respondent).

Matters called. Mr. Fitzgerald stated that he would present argument regarding the Demurrer in the Trust matter, that he would politely disagree with the Court in the Estate matter, that he would "table" the tort claims, and that he would be limiting his remarks/argument to the Trust case.

Demurrer to Probate Code Sec. 850 Petition:

The tentative ruling was adopted as follows: The demurrer is overruled as to the first three causes of action relating to the ownership of property (850, quiet title, declaratory relief). Petitioners have standing for the limited purpose of bringing the 850 petition pursuant to Probate Code section 850(a)(2)(C) as parties claiming an interest in property to which the decedent held title at the time of his death. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all remaining causes of action because Petitioners are not otherwise interested parties to this probate proceeding and have no basis to succeed to any property through probate. If the property that is the subject of the 850 petition is determined to belong to the trust, their rights will be entirely determined in the parallel trust proceeding. To the extent the property is ultimately determined to belong to this estate, Petitioners will have no rights to it or anything else in the estate. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Alesia L. Collins-Hudson, CSR #7751, of Esquire Depostion Solutions, Tel. 415.591.3333.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Discovery Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called with case no. PTR-16-299977, The Matter of D J H (Dated July 24, 1990) Trust.

Appearances: George Vausher and Michael Fitzgerald for Debra Duncan and Nicole Biddinger (Petitioners). Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Respondent/Beneficiary). John Boessnecker for Ramon Apodaca III (Respondent).

Matters called. Mr. Fitzgerald stated that he would present argument regarding the Demurrer in the Trust matter, that he would politely disagree with the Court in the Estate matter, that he would "table" the tort claims, and that he would be limiting his remarks/argument to the Trust case.

Motion to Compel Further Responses And For Discovery Sanctions:

The tentative ruling was adopted as follows: Denied with respect to the document inspection demands given that the documents have been produced (and were mailed prior to the filing of this motion). Granted as to special interrogatory 25. Objections were waived by untimely service and, in any event, the objections would be overruled. Petitioners Duncan and Biddinger have had a pleading pending in this matter continually from the time the discovery was propounded to the present. The information sought reasonably may lead to admissible evidence. In any event, objections raised for the first time in a response to a motion to compel are not appropriately considered. Petitioners Duncan and Biddinger must fully respond to this interrogatory within 15 days. Petitioners must also pay sanctions in the amount of $810 to Respondent Lewallen, reflecting the Court's determination of reasonable fees and costs to litigate the interrogatory issue as to which there was no substantial justification for failing to respond. No sanctions are allowed with respect to the document inspection issues as to which the documents were provided, and the motion was not needed to obtain that production. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Alesia L. Collins-Hudson, CSR #7751, of Esquire Depostion Solutions, Tel. 415.591.3333.

Demurrer To First Amended Objections Of Objectors Debra Duncan And Nicole Biddinger To Petition For Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Jun-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Appearing through Court Call Michael Fitzgerald for Ms. Duncan and Ms. Biddinger (Beneficiaries). Nancy Lofdahl for and with Alyson Tufts. Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Beneficiary). John Boessnecker for Ramond Apadapca.

Mr. Fitzgerald presented his oral argument in support of his opposition to the tenative ruling. Mr. Boessnecker responded with his rebuttal argument to Mr. Fitzgerald's argument. Ms. Lofdahl clarified what the assets to the estate are. Mr. Fitzgerald presented closing argument to the Court.

After hearing oral arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows. The tenative ruling is adopted. Petitioner's demurrer to objections is sustained without leave to amend. Objectors are neither beneficiaries of decedent's will nor potential intestate heirs. Objectors' interest in property claimed by the estate will be determined in the separate trust proceeding. If it is determined that the property belongs to the trust estate, there will be nothing for the decedent's estate to administer or distribute, and the admission of decedent's will would not have affected Objectors' rights. If some or all of the property is determined to belong to decedent's estate, Objectors will have no rights with respect to that property, and the management and distribution of any such property will not affect Objectors' interests. Any evidentiary effect of decedent's intention as to the relationahip of the will to the trust will be fully resolved in deciding the 850 petition. In short, while it may be prudent to put off deciding the probate petition until the 850 petition is resolved since there will be no estate to administer unless some portion of the property is found to belong to decedent's estate, the outcome of the probate petition can have no effect on either the outcome of the 850 petition or on Objectors' rights. Mr Boessnecker to provide a form of order that tracks the tenative ruling approved as to form.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Nancy Wong. Deputy: Barrientos. Not reported.
